_doc: Each rule below is a concrete mineable claim extracted by hand from a
tools/mine_skill_rules.py walks the manifest, applies each rule against
the files in its scope, and reports per-rule conformance. The manifest
is the source of truth -- adding/editing a rule does NOT require
changing the miner code, only this file.
skill       which SKILL.md the rule was extracted from
section     section heading inside that skill (provenance trail)
summary     one-line rule statement in plain words
scope       which files to scan: html_pages | js_modules | edge_fns | migrations | html_and_js
polarity    convention (pattern SHOULD appear) | anti_pattern (pattern should NOT appear)
pattern     Python regex applied to file text (after comments stripped)
pattern_flags  optional: 'i' for case-insensitive, 'm' for multiline
triggered_by   optional: only count files that ALSO match this pattern
rationale   why the rule exists; cited in the miner report
severity    critical | high | medium | low | info

rules.summary: Never define escHtml() inline on an HTML page; always source from utils.js
rules.rationale: Inline copies drift; commonly drop the ' (single-quote) escape, leaving onclick handlers XSS-vulnerable.

rules.summary: service_role JWT key must never appear in frontend HTML or JS
rules.rationale: service_role bypasses RLS entirely. The pattern requires both the role name AND a JWT-style value (eyJ prefix) to avoid false positives on prose mentioning the rule.

rules.summary: No eval() with user-supplied strings in frontend code
rules.rationale: Any eval() with user input is an immediate code-injection vector.

rules.section: Stripe Secret Key -- Edge Function Only, Never Frontend
rules.summary: Stripe sk_live_ / sk_test_ keys must never appear in frontend HTML or JS
rules.rationale: Stripe secret keys in frontend would let any visitor charge against the platform.

rules.section: Edge Function CORS -- Static Origin Is a Misconfiguration
rules.summary: Edge functions must use getCorsHeaders(req), not a hardcoded Access-Control-Allow-Origin
rules.rationale: A hardcoded origin breaks local testing (file:// sends Origin: null) and silently goes undetected on the live site.

rules.section: viewport-fit=cover is required before safe areas work
rules.summary: Every HTML page's viewport meta must include viewport-fit=cover
rules.rationale: env(safe-area-inset-*) returns 0 unless viewport-fit=cover is set -- iPhone notch + home indicator overlap content.

rules.section: Tailwind utility classes silently override wh-input font-size below 16px
rules.summary: Never apply text-sm or text-xs to a .wh-input element (triggers iOS auto-zoom)
rules.rationale: iOS Safari auto-zooms on focus when input font-size < 16px. text-sm = 14px, text-xs = 12px -- both trigger the zoom.

rules.section: PDF avoid-all Pagebreak Causes Blank First Page
rules.summary: Never use 'avoid-all' in html2pdf.js pagebreak config -- it causes blank first page
rules.rationale: avoid-all pushes the first content block to page 2, leaving page 1 blank.

rules.section: Toast containers need ARIA -- mobile screen readers depend on this
rules.summary: Toast containers must have role="alert" aria-live="polite"
rules.rationale: VoiceOver/TalkBack rely on aria-live for toast announcements. Without it, save confirmations are completely silent on mobile.

rules.section: Bump service worker cache version when ANY SHELL_FILE changes
rules.summary: sw.js must declare a versioned CACHE_NAME
rules.rationale: Without a versioned CACHE_NAME, PWA users get old cached HTML indefinitely after deploy.

rules.summary: Never use #e8920a -- not a brand color; use #FDB94A as the gradient end
rules.rationale: Color drifted into parts-tracker and assistant. The canonical orange gradient is #F7A21B -> #FDB94A.

rules.summary: Never use rgba(0,0,0,0.25) as input background -- renders darker than navy
rules.rationale: Canonical input bg is rgba(22,32,50,0.6). Black-based rgba renders noticeably darker than navy.

rules.summary: Brand orange #F7A21B should appear on every visible page (brand coherence)
rules.rationale: Canonical primary orange. Pages without it usually drift to off-brand hex codes.

rules.summary: Pages should reference Poppins font (brand typography)
rules.rationale: Canonical platform font. Pages missing it drift to system-ui or Inter.

rules.section: Missing Supabase CDN crashes ALL JS silently
rules.summary: Any HTML page calling createClient() must also load the supabase-js CDN
rules.rationale: Missing CDN = ReferenceError on first createClient call, silently kills ALL subsequent JS on the page.

rules.summary: Avoid innerHTML+= concatenation -- preferred is .innerHTML = template or appendChild
rules.rationale: innerHTML+= forces a re-parse of the entire element on every append, and is the most common XSS-by-concatenation site.

rules.summary: Avoid alert() in production code -- use showToast() instead
rules.rationale: alert() blocks the main thread, looks unprofessional, and isn't keyboard-accessible. Toast notifications are the platform convention. Pattern requires a string literal as first arg to avoid false positives on text like 'alert(s)' in template literals.

rules.summary: Pages must use escHtml() (from utils.js) -- either direct call `escHtml(` or canonical alias `const e = escHtml` + e( calls
rules.rationale: Any page that uses innerHTML to render user data must escape it. Two canonical idioms are accepted: direct escHtml() calls, or the `const e = escHtml` alias (memory rule -- shortens template literals in renderer functions).

excluded_files.reason: Static-content page: innerHTML only ever assigned literal strings from a frozen catalog (no user data flows through it). Confirmed 2026-05-19 by grep of innerHTML sites.
Static-content page: renders a fixed P&ID symbol library; innerHTML carries no user input. Confirmed 2026-05-19 by grep of innerHTML sites.

rules.summary: Pages mutating shared catalogs (assets, inventory_items, hive_members) should call writeAuditLog()
rules.rationale: Privileged mutations must be audit-logged so supervisors can trace what was approved/rejected/kicked.

rules.summary: Every edge fn imports getCorsHeaders from _shared/cors.ts
rules.rationale: Static CORS origin is always wrong; the dynamic helper handles file:// (null origin) for local testing.

excluded_files.reason: Stripe webhook receiver: Stripe servers do not send a browser Origin header and require a wildcard or absent ACAO. Using the dynamic helper would echo back a non-Stripe origin on misrouted traffic. Verified 2026-05-19 against the Stripe webhook signature flow.

rules.summary: Every edge fn responds to OPTIONS preflight
rules.rationale: Without OPTIONS handling, browsers block the actual request on CORS preflight failure.

rules.section: Missing GRANT on RLS-enabled tables causes 401
rules.summary: Migrations that enable RLS should also GRANT permissions to anon/authenticated
rules.rationale: ENABLE RLS without GRANT = 401 on every query. Supabase Studio auto-grants via GUI; SQL migrations do not. NOTE: this rule is per-file -- it does not see GRANTs added in sibling migrations. Historical migrations flagged here may already be granted via later migration files (e.g., 20260430000001_community_grants.sql, 20260511000001_agent_memory.sql, 20260518000001_security_hardening_pass.sql). Severity downgraded to medium until cross-file resolution is added.

rules.summary: Pages reading localStorage worker_name must also call restoreIdentityFromSession(db) to sync with Supabase Auth
rules.rationale: Identity migration (C1-C4) replaced string localStorage with Supabase Auth sessions. Any page still reading the localStorage worker_name must call restoreIdentityFromSession(db) on load or it diverges from the canonical auth.uid() identity. Surfaced by AI extraction 2026-05-18, manually reviewed.

rules.summary: Functions declared SECURITY DEFINER should also SET search_path
rules.rationale: SECURITY DEFINER without search_path lets a malicious schema shadow public functions (Postgres CVE class). NOTE: per-file rule -- historical migrations flagged here may have their functions retroactively re-declared (with SET search_path) in later migrations like 20260511000002_db_hygiene_batch.sql or ALTER FUNCTION'd in 20260518000001_security_hardening_pass.sql. The runtime DB reflects the latest definition. Severity downgraded to medium until cross-migration awareness is added.

rules.summary: Avoid .innerHTML inside a .forEach loop (perf + XSS risk)
rules.rationale: innerHTML inside a loop re-parses on every iteration AND is the most common XSS-by-concatenation site. Build a single template string, then assign innerHTML once.

rules.summary: Never use the Function() constructor with user input
rules.rationale: new Function() is eval()'s twin: any user-controlled string passed in becomes executable code.

rules.summary: Avoid storing tokens / secrets / api_keys directly in localStorage
rules.rationale: localStorage is readable by any script on the page (XSS exfiltration vector). Tokens should live in HttpOnly cookies or short-lived Supabase session state.

rules.summary: Cards must use border-radius: 1rem, not 1.25rem (designer skill drift point)
rules.rationale: Logbook form card drifted to 1.25rem; canonical card radius is 1rem. Designer skill explicitly flags 1.25rem as a known drift.

rules.summary: Every visible page should have a <main> landmark for screen-reader navigation
rules.rationale: <main> is the WAI-ARIA landmark that lets keyboard / screen-reader users skip nav and jump to content. Pages without it force linear-scan navigation.

rules.summary: Every tag should have an alt attribute (use alt="" for decorative)
rules.rationale: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.1.1: non-decorative images need a textual alternative. Decorative ones still need alt="" so screen readers skip them.

rules.summary: Use .classList.add/remove/toggle, not .className= (avoids overwriting other classes)
rules.rationale: .className= replaces ALL classes -- if another script added a state class (like .hidden), it gets wiped. classList methods are surgical.

rules.section: utils.js must load BEFORE the main script block
rules.summary: Pages that call escHtml/getDb must load utils.js before the main inline <script> block
rules.rationale: If utils.js loads AFTER the inline script that calls escHtml/getDb, the call throws ReferenceError and silently kills the entire <script> block.

rules.section: No Em Dashes in AI Prompt Template Strings
rules.summary: Edge fns should not put em-dashes in callAI / system_prompt template strings
rules.rationale: Em-dashes degrade answer-extraction quality in downstream LLM consumers (Perplexity, ChatGPT). Use colons, commas, or restructure.

rules.section: Two-store KG scope split (HIVE vs PLATFORM)
rules.summary: If voice-handler.js queries the hive-scoped KG RPC, it must also query the platform-scoped one
rules.rationale: Standards-derived KG facts live in platform_knowledge_graph_facts (single source of truth); hive-specific facts live in knowledge_graph_facts. The voice handler must merge both shelves so workers get canon + hive citations. Mirrors the kb_chunks / industry_standards_chunks precedent. Regression caught 2026-05-19: 1,533 standards facts had been broadcast across 3 hives (4,605 rows); split fixed the duplication and any future drop of the platform RPC would silently lose canon citations.

rules.section: Two-store KG scope split (HIVE vs PLATFORM)
rules.summary: No migration may broadcast platform-canon rows across hives via CROSS JOIN hives into knowledge_graph_facts
rules.rationale: Broadcasting standards-canon to all hives triplicates content and creates drift risk. Platform-scoped facts belong in platform_knowledge_graph_facts (no hive_id). The audit reflex (feedback_canonical_audit_reflex.md) should have caught this on the first build; this rule locks it in for future migrations.

rules.section: List View Contract -- Empty State Anchor
rules.summary: Pages that render dynamic lists must declare an empty-state element (id="empty-state" or class="empty-state")
rules.rationale: Canonical from logbook.html / inventory.html: every list page declares <div id="empty-state" class="... empty-state"> for the zero-data state. Without it, users see a blank panel with no explanation. Identified 2026-05-19 during list-display uniformity audit.

rules.section: List View Contract -- No-Results State for Filterable Lists
rules.summary: Filterable list pages (with a search input) must declare a distinct id="no-results" element separate from empty-state
rules.rationale: Canonical from logbook.html:969 + inventory.html:363: empty-state covers "never had data", no-results covers "filters returned zero". Conflating them hides the difference between onboarding state and a wrong filter. Identified 2026-05-19 during list-display uniformity audit.

rules.section: List View Contract -- Client-Side Slice Pagination
rules.summary: Dynamic-list pages should expose a Load More control (id ending in -load-more-wrap, or btn-load-more class, or a loadMoreX() handler)
rules.rationale: Frontend SKILL.md:1003 documents the Client-Side Slice Pagination pattern (loadMoreX + _displayCount). Lists that grow past ~20 rows without pagination dump the entire DOM at once -- slow render, no progressive scan. Canonical wrappers: logbook-load-more-wrap, inv-load-more-wrap, pm-load-more-wrap. Identified 2026-05-19 during list-display uniformity audit.

rules.section: List View Contract -- Filter Tabs A11y Roles
rules.summary: Filter tabs and chip rows must declare role="tablist" on the container and role="tab" + aria-selected on each button
rules.rationale: Canonical from marketplace.html:589 (section-tabs) and audit-log.html:188 (filter-row + chip + aria-selected). Screen readers cannot navigate tab UIs without tablist/tab role + aria-selected. A visual tab without these roles is a keyboard-trap regression. Identified 2026-05-19 during list-display uniformity audit.

rules.section: List View Contract -- Search Resets Display Count
rules.summary: Pages with both a search input AND client-side pagination must reset the display count on search input
rules.rationale: Frontend SKILL.md:1023 -- when a user types in the search box, the display count must reset to its starting value, otherwise the visible slice can hide new matches. Pattern: `searchInput.addEventListener('input', () => { _displayCount = 20; renderList(); });`. Identified 2026-05-19 during list-display uniformity audit.
